content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN vertices, rooted at vertex 111. The iii-th vertex of this tree has the integer AiA_iAi‚Äč written on it.

For each vertex uuu (1‚Č§u‚Č§N1 \leq u \leq N1‚Č§u‚Č§N), find the number of divisors of the product of all AvA_vAv‚Äč such that vvv lies in the subtree of uuu.

Formally, for each uuu:


Let SuS_uSu‚Äč denote the set of vertices that lie in the subtree of uuu (including uuu).
Define Xu=‚ąŹv‚ąąSuAvX_u = \prod_{v \in S_u} A_vXu‚Äč=‚ąŹv‚ąąSu‚Äč‚ÄčAv‚Äč
Find the number of divisors of XuX_uXu‚Äč
The answer may be large, so report it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input contains an integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the number of vertices.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč ‚ÄĒ the values written on the vertices.
The next N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines describe the edges of the tree. The iii-th of these N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui‚Äč and viv_ivi‚Äč, denoting an edge between vertices uiu_iui‚Äč and viv_ivi‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output one line containing NNN space-separated integers. The iii-th of these integers is the answer for vertex iii.
constraints: The edges given in the input describe a tree.
The sum of NNN across all test cases won't exceed 3‚čÖ1043 \cdot 10^43‚čÖ104.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The answers are as follows:

For u=2u = 2u=2, Xu=101X_u = 101Xu‚Äč=101 which is prime and so has two factors.
For u=4u = 4u=4, Xu=103X_u = 103Xu‚Äč=103 which is prime and so has two factors.
For u=3u = 3u=3, Xu=102X_u = 102Xu‚Äč=102 which has 888 factors, namely {1,2,3,6,17,34,51,102}\{1, 2, 3, 6, 17, 34, 51, 102\}{1,2,3,6,17,34,51,102}.
For u=1u = 1u=1, Xu=106110600X_u = 106110600Xu‚Äč=106110600, which has 192192192 factors.
